content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 22 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 111v–111r | **Source Images:** DEU_022_page_001.jpg, DEU_022_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 156–157 | **LXX Images:** DEU_022_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_022_vaticanus_page_002.jpg

---

**1** You shall not see the ox of your brother or his sheep straying, and hide yourself from them; you shall certainly return them to your brother.

**2** And if your brother is not near to you, or you do not know him, then you shall gather it into the midst of your house, and it shall be with you until your brother inquires for it, and you shall return it to him.

**3** And so you shall do with his donkey, and so you shall do with his garment, and so you shall do with every lost thing of your brother that he loses and you find—you are not permitted to hide yourself.

**4** You shall not see the donkey of your brother or his ox falling on the road and hide yourself from them; you shall certainly raise them up with him.

**5** A woman shall not put on the apparel of a man, and a man shall not wear a woman's garment; for anyone who does these things is an abomination to the LORD your God.

**6** If a bird's nest happens to be before you on the road, in any tree or on the ground—fledglings or eggs—with the mother sitting on the fledglings or on the eggs, you shall not take the mother with the young.

**7** You shall certainly send away the mother, and the young you may take for yourself, so that it may go well for you and you may prolong your days.

**8** When you build a new house, you shall make a parapet for your roof, and you shall not bring blood-guilt upon your house if anyone should fall from it.

**9** You shall not sow your vineyard with two kinds [kilayim]; lest the fullness of the seed that you have sown and the yield of the vineyard become holy [for the sanctuary].

**10** You shall not plow with an ox and a donkey together.

**11** You shall not wear a mingled thing [sha'atnez]—wool and linen together.

**12** You shall make yourself tassels [gedilim] on the four corners of your garment with which you cover yourself.

**13** If a man takes a wife and goes in to her and hates her,

**14** and he places against her a [charge of] disgraceful things and brings out a bad name against her, and says: I took this woman, and when I drew near to her I did not find in her the tokens of virginity [betulim]—

**15** then the father of the young woman and her mother shall take and bring out the tokens of the young woman's virginity to the elders of the city at the gate.

**16** And the father of the young woman shall say to the elders: I gave my daughter to this man as a wife, and he has hated her;

**17** and behold, he has placed [a charge of] disgraceful things, saying: I did not find in your daughter the tokens of virginity. Yet these are the tokens of my daughter's virginity. And they shall spread the garment before the elders of the city.

**18** And the elders of that city shall take the man and discipline him,

**19** and they shall fine him one hundred [pieces] of silver and give them to the father of the young woman, because he brought out a bad name against a virgin of Yisra'el; and she shall be his wife—he may not send her away all his days.

**20** But if this matter is true—the tokens of virginity were not found for the young woman—

**21** then they shall bring out the young woman to the entrance of her father's house, and the men of her city shall stone her with stones, and she shall die; for she has done a disgraceful thing in Yisra'el, to commit prostitution in her father's house. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

**22** If a man is found lying with a woman who is a wife of a husband [ba'alat ba'al], then both of them shall die—the man who lay with the woman and the woman as well. So you shall purge the evil from Yisra'el.

**23** If there is a girl [na'arah] who is a virgin, betrothed to a man, and a man finds her in the city and lies with her—

**24** then you shall bring both of them out to the gate of that city and stone them with stones, and they shall die: the girl because she did not cry out in the city, and the man because he humbled his neighbor's wife. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

**25** But if in the open field the man finds the betrothed girl, and the man seizes her and lies with her, then only the man who lay with her shall die.

**26** And to the girl you shall do nothing; the girl has no sin worthy of death, for this matter is like a man rising against his neighbor and killing him.

**27** For he found her in the open field; the betrothed girl cried out, but there was no one to save her.

**28** If a man finds a girl [na'arah] who is a virgin, who is not betrothed, and seizes her and lies with her, and they are found—

**29** then the man who lay with her shall give to the father of the girl fifty [pieces] of silver, and she shall be his wife, because he humbled her; he may not send her away all his days.

**30** A man shall not take his father's wife, and shall not uncover his father's skirt.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**v.5 "apparel of a man"** — Hebrew *keli gever*, lit. "vessel/implement of a man." The term *keli* covers tools, weapons, and garments; the prohibition is broader than dress alone. EMET renders "apparel" for readability while retaining "man" (*gever*, the strong/valiant man, not the generic *ish*).

**v.9 "kilayim"** — Hebrew *kilayim* means "two kinds" (a mixed planting). EMET preserves the transliteration with bracket only for the clause gloss. The result of violation is that the "fullness" (*melei*) is consecrated—forfeited to the sanctuary.

**v.11 "sha'atnez"** — The term *sha'atnez* (unique Hebrew hapax with possible Egyptian loanword roots) refers specifically to wool-linen mixture. EMET renders "mingled thing [sha'atnez]" with the Hebrew preserved.

**v.12 "tassels [gedilim]"** — Here the term is *gedilim* (twisted/braided things), distinct from *tsitsit* (Num 15:38). EMET notes both terms are used for the tassel commandment; here the Deut 22 term is *gedilim*.

**v.14 "tokens of virginity [betulim]"** — Hebrew *betulim* (plural) denotes the physical evidence of virginity, commonly understood as bloodstained cloth from the wedding night. EMET preserves the term with its gloss rather than a clinical paraphrase.

**v.22 "wife of a husband [ba'alat ba'al]"** — Lit. "mistress of a master/husband." The doubled root *ba'al* (both noun and implied owner) is significant: she is a woman owned/governed by a husband. EMET renders the phrase to expose its literal structure.

**v.25–27 Distinction between city and field** — The MT distinction is juridical: crying out in the city would summon help; in the field there is no one to save her. The LXX (Vaticanus p. 156–157) preserves this distinction faithfully.

**v.30 "uncover his father's skirt"** — Hebrew *lo yegaleh kenaf aviv*, a euphemism for illicit sexual relations with the stepmother. EMET renders literally as the euphemistic idiom is used consistently in the OT (cf. Ruth 3:9; Ezek 16:8).

**LXX comparison** — Vaticanus p. 157 begins with material from v.22 onward. The LXX consistently reads *pais* ("girl/child") for *na'arah*, and *gynaikas andros* ("a man's woman") for *ba'alat ba'al*, both closely reflecting MT meaning. No significant additions or omissions detected on manuscript examination.

*— EMET*
